channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Gabbard: Zelensky’s ‘immediate escalation’ during WH meeting created ‘huge rift’ in relationship
item.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard said Ukrainian President Zelensky’s “immediate escalation” during the White House meeting with President Trump created a “huge rift” in the relationship between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Outrage as Israel cuts off Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel faced sharp criticism on Sunday as it stopped the entry of all food and other supplies into Gaza on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” if Hamas doesn’t accept a new proposal to extend a fragile ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sanders says it’s a 'horrible suggestion' for Zelensky to step down
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) said it’s a “horrible suggestion” for Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky to step down after his disastrous meeting with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: US aircraft carrier arrives in South Korea after North Korea test-fired missiles
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A U.S. aircraft carrier arrived in South Korea on Sunday in a show of force, days after North Korea test-launched cruise missiles to demonstrate its counterattack capabilities.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Francis off mechanical ventilation entirely, signalling he's overcome risks from respiratory crisis
item.description: The Vatican says Pope Francis has remained in stable condition and didn’t need any mechanical ventilation.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Uruguay's new leftist president takes office, facing a financial balancing act
item.description: MONTEVIDEO, Uruguay (AP) — Yamandú Orsi, a telegenic left-leaning former mayor and history teacher, took office as Uruguay's new president on Saturday, at the helm of a government that has pledged to strengthen the social safety net while reversing years of economic stagnation.
item.dc:creator: MATILDE CAMPODONICO and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK's Starmer says Europe is at crossroads in history as world leaders discuss ending war in Ukraine
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer says Britain will use 1.6 billion pounds ($2 billion) in export financing to supply 5,000 air defense missiles for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK, Ukraine sign loan agreement after 'meaningful and warm' visit
item.description: The United Kingdom agreed to loan Ukraine around $3 billion on Saturday to support their efforts in the war against Russia after a contentious White House meeting left the world wondering about the nation’s ability to secure its sovereign borders.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vatican gives health update as Pope enters third week in hospital
item.description: Pope Francis' condition is stable, and he is alternating between mechanical ventilation and high-flow oxygen therapy, the Vatican said on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainians rally around Zelenskyy as defender of national interest after Oval Office blowout
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Soon after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy left the White House on Friday after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with President Donald Trump, Ukrainians rallied around Zelenskyy as a defender of his country's interests.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands of mourners attend funeral of Taliban-linked cleric killed in Pakistan suicide blast
item.description: AKORA KHATTAK, Pakistan (AP) — Thousands of mourners attended a funeral on Saturday for a Taliban-linked cleric slain in a suicide bomb attack on a seminary in Pakistan’s northwest.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope had coughing fit, inhaled vomit and now requires assisted ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered an isolated coughing fit on Friday that resulted in him inhaling vomit and requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying an alarming setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 7 key takeaways from the Israeli military's report on what happened on Oct. 7
item.description: The Israeli military's assessment goes through the years, months and hours leading up to Hamas' brutal surprise attacks on Oct. 7, 2023. This includes intelligence and operational assessments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope suffers breathing crisis amid pneumonia battle, Vatican says
item.description: Following the episode, the pope began non-invasive ventilation and was responding well, according to the Vatican.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Zelenskyy's White House meeting with Trump, Vance turns to shouting match
item.description: A White House meeting that was intended to kick off negotiations over a deal that would offer the United States access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals unraveled Friday afternoon as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy clashed with President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance in the Oval Of
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and JUSTIN SPIKE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bangladeshi students who led uprising that ousted ex-premier Sheikh Hasina form new political party
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Students in Bangladesh who led a mass uprising to topple former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina last summer are now diving into politics by forming the new National Citizen Party. The aim is to create new political space in a fiercely divisive dynastic political landscape.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suicide bomb at a seminary in northwest Pakistan kills top cleric and 5 others ahead of Ramadan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — An attacker blew himself up Friday in a mosque at a pro-Taliban seminary in northwestern Pakistan, killing a top cleric and five other worshippers and wounding dozens ahead of the fasting month of Ramadan, police said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Morocco urges people to not buy sheep for Eid al-Adha celebrations
item.description: OULAD SLAMA, Morocco (AP) — Sheep come running when Larbi El Ghazouani pours alfalfa and straw into their troughs twice a day.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ and AKRAM OUBACHIR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Golden toilet was stolen from English palace in 'audacious raid,' prosecutor says
item.description: It was not your typical smash-and-grab burglary and the booty was precious: a toilet worth more than its weight in gold.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: French surgeon accused of raping or abusing 299 people, mostly child patients
item.description: VANNES, France (AP) — A former surgeon went on trial in France on Monday for the alleged rape or sexual abuse of 299 victims, most of them children who were his patients, in what investigators and his own notebooks describe as a pattern of violence spanning over three decades.
item.dc:creator: JADE LE DELEY and NICOLAS VAUX-MONTAGNY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets on Sunday for the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, Hassan Nasrallah, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, ABBY SEWELL and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: More 'city-killer' asteroids apparent, but fret not: astronomer
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation
